reasons.title: Zero Entitlement Risk
reasons.description: The project is fully permitted, with all impact fees paid by the current developer. Combined with a concise 16-month construction timeline, this removes massive entitlement risk and accelerates the path to stabilization.

reasons.title: Major Local Incentives
reasons.description: The project economics are heavily bolstered by a secured $775,000 city grant for construction costs, alongside a 75% TIF reimbursement on property taxes for the first five years post-completion.

reasons.title: Prime Walkable Location
reasons.description: Situated on a 1.6-acre site directly overlooking Lake Wire, the development offers walkable access to downtown Lakeland and the transformative 168-acre Bonnet Springs Park.

summary.quote: The finished product will include a highly amenitized four story closed corridor building with elevator, which will be superior to other projects in the submarket.
paragraphs: We are proud to introduce an 90 unit fully permitted urban infill project consisting of one 4-story, closed corridor building with one- and two-bedroom units averaging 826 square feet, many of which will directly overlook Lake Wire. Included on the site is a two-tier parking deck and secondary structure adjacent to the resort style pool. In addition, the site is located in a Qualified Opportunity Zone (QOZ).

Lake Wire Apartments is situated on a 1.6-acre, seven parcel assemblage located on Peachtree St., directly south of George Jenkins Blvd. and directly north of Lake Wire. Within walking distance of the site, residents will have access to Freedom Park, a large greenspace overlooking Lake Wire, as well as a track-like sidewalk surrounding Lake Wire which stretches nearly a mile. In addition, residents will be within walking distance of the new Bonnet Springs Park development.
summary.conclusion: By combining shovel-ready status, a prime lakefront location, and robust public financial incentives, Lake Wire Apartments represents an exceptional opportunity to deliver a premium multifamily asset in a thriving Central Florida hub.

cards.title: Financial Returns
cards.summary: The project targets aggressive risk-adjusted returns through secured and hard-to-do HUD 221(d)(4) financing, significant non-dilutive municipal incentives including TIF and cash grants.

cards.title: Property Overview
cards.summary: A fully-permitted urban infill Class A development featuring a resort-style pool, structured parking, direct exterior access for first-floor units, and high-end finishes including quartz countertops, stainless steel appliances, wood-style plank flooring, and keyless entry.

cards.title: Market Analysis
cards.summary: Lakeland is a pivotal hub for commerce between Tampa and Orlando, currently emerging as a rising star in Florida's tech and logistics sectors with robust population growth.

cards.title: Sponsor Profile
cards.summary: Led by Tim McCoskey and Tomas Possenti, Sun South Development brings over 35 years of experience and a flawless track record across 7,000 units of multifamily and commercial development.

financialReturns.pageSubtitle: Detailed financial projections and investment structure

projections.label: Target IRR
projections.description: Targeted project-level Internal Rate of Return over 5-year hold period.

projections.label: Investor Equity Multiple
projections.description: Anticipated Multiple on Invested Capital for investors at exit.

projections.label: Stabilized Yield (ROC)
projections.description: Stabilized Yield on Cost (Net Operating Income / Total Project Cost).

projections.label: Avg. Cash-on-Cash Return
projections.description: Average annual cash distribution relative to initial equity investment.

projections.label: Preferred Return
projections.description: Contractual preferred return to investors prior to profit split.

projections.label: Total Project Cost
projections.description: The total capital required to fund development, land, and carry costs.

uses.description: Interest reserves and HUD financing fees

timeline.description: Construction period (estimated 16 months) and initial lease-up (4 months).
Stabilized operations with 70% of available cash flow distributed to investors.
Sale of the property at a 6.0% exit cap rate with 70% of profit proceeds to investors.

benefits.title: Capital Gains Deferral
benefits.description: Investors can defer capital gains taxes on original gains invested through 12/31/2026.

benefits.title: Tax-Free Appreciation
benefits.description: 100% exclusion of capital gains on the new investment if held for at least 10 years.

benefits.title: 179D Energy Tax Deduction
benefits.description: Estimated energy efficiency deductions between $251k–$490k available for the project.

benefits.title: TIF Incentive Benefit
benefits.description: 75% reduction in real estate taxes for the first five years after obtaining Certificate of Occupancy.

saleWaterfall.description: Full repayment of the HUD 221(d)(4) loan balance.
Return of initial capital contributions to LP and GP.
Remaining profit proceeds (Carried Interest) split between LP and GP.

cashFlowDistribution.description: Repayment of operating expenses and debt service.
Cash flow available for distribution split between LP and GP.

highlights.title: Proximity to Bonnet Springs Park
highlights.description: Walking distance to the new 180-acre mixed-use development featuring a children's museum, botanical gardens, and attractions.

features: Publix Field at Joker Marchant Stadium

timeline.description: Permits fully approved by the City of Lakeland and paid for by the current developer. New SPE for acquisition and clean PSA established.

timeline.title: Construction
timeline.description: Estimated construction timeline of sixteen (16) months for the 4-story building and parking deck. Senior debt via HUD 221(d)(4).

paragraphs: Sun South Development (SSD) is a vertically integrated real estate development firm led by Tim McCoskey and Tomas Possenti. The firm specializes in complex urban infill, luxury multifamily, and mixed-use projects, with a specific expertise in HUD 221(d)(4) financing and Opportunity Zone developments.

The firm's principals bring a multi-disciplinary approach, combining backgrounds in architecture, appraisal (MAI designation), and general contracting to deliver value-driven projects that prioritize sustainability and quality. SSD is currently executing the Lake Wire Apartments, a 90-unit project in Lakeland, FL. The team boasts a flawless track record qualifying under strict HUD 221(d)(4) financing and has developed over 7,000 units career-to-date.

partners.name: Sun South Development
description: Lead Sponsor/Developer responsible for overall execution, entitlement, and HUD financing.

partners.name: MEC Development Associates
description: Development partner specializing in South Florida residential and commercial oversight.

partners.name: Charter Realty Partners
description: Real estate consultancy advising on ground-up development, multifamily, and mixed-use projects.

whyItMatters: Vertical integration ensures control over costs from permitting to construction.
HUD experience provides access to high-leverage, non-recourse senior debt.
Strong local government relationships secured $775k in grants and 75% TIF.

metrics.label: Total Units Developed
metrics.description: Total residential units developed over the principals' careers.

metrics.label: Years of Experience
metrics.description: The principal (Tim McCoskey) has been active in development and construction since 1985.

metrics.label: HUD Success Rate
metrics.description: Historical success rate maintaining zero defaults or failed projects under HUD underwriting.

metrics.label: Commercial Development
metrics.description: Square footage of commercial and warehouse space developed.

teamMembers.name: Tim McCoskey
teamMembers.title: Owner / President
teamMembers.background: Mr. McCoskey started in real estate development and construction in 1985. He has developed and built approximately 7,000 units. He holds a Florida state certified General Contractor license, a Florida Real Estate Broker license, and previously obtained an MAI designation as a commercial appraiser.

teamMembers.name: Tomas Possenti
teamMembers.title: Partner
teamMembers.background: With over 15 years of experience in real estate investment, development, and property management, Tomas has evaluated over $2 billion in distressed assets. He also serves as a Partner at MEC Development Associates and Charter Realty Partners.

partners.name: Charlan Brock Architects
partners.description: Lead architectural firm for the Lake Wire project.

partners.name: Langan Engineering
partners.description: Engineering lead for site design and structural requirements.

advantages.title: Vertically Integrated Leadership
advantages.description: Principals hold Florida General Contractor and Real Estate Broker licenses, managing the entire lifecycle from design and permitting to construction and management.

advantages.title: HUD Underwriting Specialist
advantages.description: The sponsor maintains a 'flawless' track record required for HUD 221(d)(4) financing, which has no tolerance for failed projects or past financial blunders.

advantages.title: Data-Driven Underwriting
advantages.description: With a background including the MAI designation, the sponsor utilizes a 'Highest and Best Use' valuation framework to ensure conservative underwriting and project viability.

advantages.title: Strategic Regional Expertise
advantages.description: Decades of experience in the Florida market, specifically navigating distressed assets and complex urban infill entitlements in South Florida and Lakeland.

steps.title: Review Documentation
points: Examine the Lake Wire Offering Memorandum.
Review the 'About Us' track record and past performance data.

steps.title: Consult with Principals
points: Schedule a walkthrough of the conservative proforma assumptions.
Request an in-depth comp review if required for underwriting.

steps.title: Finalize Commitment
points: Review the clean Purchase and Sale Agreement (PSA).
Evaluate the new Special Purpose Entity (SPE) established for acquisition.

steps.title: Fund Investment
points: Invest through the Qualified Opportunity Fund (QOF).
Secure tax deferral benefits through the 12/31/2026 deadline.
